title: Dense Korean Flaxseed Juk
description: A hearty, slow-cooked Korean comfort food made with nutritious flaxseed, ideal for a quick fix on a weeknight. This traditional Gyeongsang Province dish is a staple for its rich, savory flavor and dense texture.
instructions: Add the flaxseed and water to a large pot and whisk to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and let simmer for 20 minutes.
Mince the dried ginger and grind the garlic cloves in a mortar and pestle or with a microplane grater.
Add the ginger, garlic, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Heat the sesame oil in a small pan over medium heat and pour it over the juk.
Stir to combine and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Serve the juk hot, garnished with chopped scallions or sesame seeds if desired.
